cases.title: Create a TestBench from an empty bench slot using a discovered spec
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled in settings
At least one bench slot is empty
The focused project repo contains at least one file matching .specifications/*/test-cases.json
User is on the bench list view for the project

steps.instruction: Open the option menu on an empty bench slot
Click 'Create a TestBench' from the option menu

observations.expected: Spec-picker modal opens
'Create a TestBench' option is present in the menu

steps.instruction: Observe the discovered-specs list in the modal

observations.expected: Each discovered spec row shows slug, file path, and case count
At least one row matches a .specifications/*/test-cases.json file in the repo

steps.instruction: Click a spec row to select it

observations.expected: Row is highlighted as selected
Create button becomes enabled

steps.instruction: Click the Create button

observations.expected: Modal closes
Bench is created with a worktree bound to the selected spec
Bench detail view opens

steps.instruction: Observe the tabs in the bench detail view

observations.expected: A 'TestBench' tab is the first tab with an amber accent dot
Standard tabs (Components, Terminal, Info) are also present
Inspection tab is present if previously configured

steps.instruction: Click the TestBench tab

observations.expected: TestBench content loads
The focused spec slug and path are displayed
The results panel is visible

cases.title: Create TestBench option is absent when the feature is disabled
preconditions: TestBench feature is disabled in settings
At least one bench slot is empty
User is on the bench list view

steps.instruction: Open the option menu on an empty bench slot

observations.expected: 'Create a TestBench' option is NOT present
Normal bench setup options remain visible

cases.title: Spec-picker modal lists all discovered test-cases.json files from the repo
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
The repo contains .specifications/alpha/test-cases.json, .specifications/beta/test-cases.json, .specifications/gamma/test-cases.json
User has opened the Create a TestBench modal

steps.instruction: Observe the discovered-specs list

observations.expected: Exactly three rows: alpha, beta, gamma
Each row shows slug, relative path, and case count
No rows from outside .specifications/ are shown

cases.title: Create button is disabled until a spec is selected
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
User has opened the Create a TestBench modal
No spec is selected and the manual-path input is empty

steps.instruction: Observe the Create button state

observations.expected: Create button is visually disabled and cannot be clicked

steps.instruction: Click the Create button without making a selection

observations.expected: No bench is created
Modal remains open

steps.instruction: Select a spec row from the discovered list

observations.expected: Create button becomes enabled

cases.title: TestBench tab appears first and all standard bench tabs are retained
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
A TestBench has been created and bound to a spec
The bench has Components, Terminal, Inspection, and Info tabs configured

steps.instruction: Navigate to the bench detail view for the TestBench

observations.expected: The first tab is 'TestBench' with an amber accent dot
Components, Terminal, Inspection, and Info tabs are present
Tab order is TestBench, then the existing tabs in their original order

steps.instruction: Click each standard tab (Components, Terminal, Inspection, Info)

observations.expected: Each tab opens and displays its normal content without interference from TestBench

cases.title: Create a TestBench with a valid manual file path
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
An empty bench slot exists
A valid test-cases.json exists at a known path within the registered project repo
User has opened the Create a TestBench modal

steps.instruction: Locate the manual-path input field in the modal
Type a valid path to a test-cases.json within the project repo

observations.expected: Input shows a validating indicator while the path is validated

steps.instruction: Wait for validation to complete

observations.expected: Input shows a green check (valid state)
Create button becomes enabled
No error message is shown

steps.instruction: Click the Create button

observations.expected: Modal closes
TestBench is created bound to the manually specified spec
Bench detail opens with the TestBench tab showing the correct spec path

cases.title: Re-point TestBench to a different spec and preserve prior results independently
preconditions: A TestBench is bound to spec-A
At least one result has been recorded against spec-A on this bench
Spec-B is discoverable in the same project repo
User is on the TestBench tab

steps.instruction: Click the 'Change focused spec' (re-point) action in the TestBench tab header
Observe the spec-picker modal

observations.expected: Modal opens
Current spec (spec-A) is shown as active
Other discovered specs including spec-B are listed

steps.instruction: Select spec-B from the discovered list

observations.expected: Spec-B row is highlighted
Confirm button becomes enabled

steps.instruction: Confirm the re-point action

observations.expected: Modal closes
TestBench header now shows spec-B as the focused spec
Staleness is re-evaluated against the newly focused plan

steps.instruction: Re-open the spec-picker and switch back to spec-A

observations.expected: Spec-A results recorded before the re-point are fully preserved and displayed
No results from spec-B are mixed into spec-A's result set

cases.title: Spec-picker shows an empty state when no .specifications/ test-cases.json files exist
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
The repo contains no files matching .specifications/*/test-cases.json
An empty bench slot exists
User has opened the Create a TestBench modal

steps.instruction: Observe the discovered-specs list

observations.expected: An explicit empty-state message is shown (e.g. 'No spec files found in .specifications/')
The manual-path input is still available
Create remains disabled until a valid manual path is entered

cases.title: Manual path outside the registered project repo is rejected, including traversal
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
The registered project repo is at /home/user/projects/myrepo
A valid test-cases.json exists outside the registered repo
User has opened the Create a TestBench modal

steps.instruction: Enter an out-of-repo path (/home/user/other-repo/.specifications/suite/test-cases.json)

observations.expected: Validation fails with a red helper indicating the path must be within the registered repo
Input shows invalid state
Create remains disabled

steps.instruction: Attempt traversal using /../ to escape the repo root

observations.expected: The canonicalized path is rejected as outside the repo root
A red helper error is shown
Create remains disabled

cases.title: Manual path to a file that fails schema validation is rejected
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
A file inside the repo contains JSON that fails the published test-cases.json schema
User has opened the Create a TestBench modal

steps.instruction: Enter the path to the schema-invalid file

observations.expected: Input shows a validating state briefly

steps.instruction: Wait for validation to complete

observations.expected: Input transitions to the invalid/red state
Red helper text explains the file does not conform to the schema
Create remains disabled

cases.title: Re-point is explicit: dismissing the picker does not change the focused spec
preconditions: A TestBench is bound to spec-A
User is on the TestBench tab

steps.instruction: Click the 'Change focused spec' action

observations.expected: The spec-picker opens; a confirmed selection is required before any change takes effect

steps.instruction: Close the spec-picker without selecting a different spec (cancel)

observations.expected: The TestBench still shows spec-A
No change is made to the bench binding

cases.title: REST endpoint: POST creates a TestBench and returns the expected shape
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
An empty bench slot exists
A valid spec path is known within the registered repo

steps.instruction: Send POST /api/projects/:projectId/benches with body { variant: 'testbench', focusedSpecPath: '<valid-path>' }

observations.expected: HTTP 201 Created
Response includes bench id, worktree path, variant 'testbench', and the focused spec path
GET of the bench returns variant 'testbench'

cases.title: REST endpoint: GET discover specs returns all .specifications/*/test-cases.json
preconditions: The repo contains .specifications/suite-a/test-cases.json and .specifications/suite-b/test-cases.json
TestBench feature is enabled

steps.instruction: Send GET /api/projects/:projectId/testbench/specs

observations.expected: HTTP 200 OK
Response is an array of two spec objects each with slug, path, caseCount
Slugs are 'suite-a' and 'suite-b'

cases.title: REST endpoint: PUT re-point focused spec updates the binding
preconditions: A TestBench is bound to spec-A
Spec-B is a valid discoverable spec in the same project

steps.instruction: Send PUT /api/projects/:projectId/benches/:id/testbench/focus with body { focusedSpecPath: '<spec-B-path>' }

observations.expected: HTTP 200 OK
Response shows the focused spec updated to spec-B
GET of the bench confirms spec-B
Spec-A's results file is untouched

cases.title: Reviewer reaches first recorded observation within 5 interactions from Create a TestBench
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
An empty bench slot exists
At least one spec is discoverable
User is on the bench list view

steps.instruction: Interaction 1: open the empty-slot menu and click 'Create a TestBench'

observations.expected: Spec-picker opens with discovered specs listed

steps.instruction: Interaction 2: click a spec row to select it

observations.expected: Row is selected; Create is enabled

steps.instruction: Interaction 3: click Create

observations.expected: Bench is created; bench detail opens with the TestBench tab active

steps.instruction: Interaction 4: click a case row to open its observations

observations.expected: The observation controls are shown for that case

steps.instruction: Interaction 5: mark an observation pass

observations.expected: The mark is saved and reflected in the case
First recorded observation is reached in exactly 5 interactions, satisfying NFR-007

cases.title: TestBench create flow is fully operable via keyboard
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
An empty bench slot exists
At least one spec is discoverable

steps.instruction: Using keyboard only, focus the empty-slot option-menu trigger

observations.expected: The trigger is reachable and focusable

steps.instruction: Open the menu and activate 'Create a TestBench' via keyboard

observations.expected: Spec-picker opens and focus moves into the modal

steps.instruction: Navigate the discovered-specs list and select a row via keyboard

observations.expected: A spec row is selected; focus is managed within the list

steps.instruction: Move to the Create button and activate it via keyboard

observations.expected: Bench is created
Focus moves to the bench detail
No focus trap remains in the modal

cases.title: No TestBench tab appears on a normal (non-variant) bench
preconditions: TestBench feature is enabled
A standard (non-TestBench-variant) bench exists for the project

steps.instruction: Navigate to the bench detail view for the standard bench

observations.expected: No 'TestBench' tab is present
Standard tabs (Components, Terminal, Info, and Inspection if configured) are shown as normal

cases.title: Review UI renders the grouped case list and progress bar on open
preconditions: A plan with at least one case per level (L1-L4) is loaded
The review tab is accessible

steps.instruction: Navigate to the TestBench review tab for the loaded plan
Observe the left-pane case list

observations.expected: Cases are grouped by level/priority with visible group headings
Each row shows a status indicator (dot and label)
Raw JSON is not exposed

steps.instruction: Observe the header area

observations.expected: A segmented progress bar (passed/failed/open) is visible
Mono counts are displayed
Overall progress reflects the initial not_started state

cases.title: Case detail shows full metadata, preconditions, steps, and observations
preconditions: A plan is loaded with a case that has preconditions, multiple steps, and expected observations per step
The review UI is open

steps.instruction: Click a case row in the left pane
Observe the right detail pane

observations.expected: Case title is displayed
Case id, level, and priority are shown
Preconditions list is rendered in order
Ordered steps are displayed sequentially
Each step shows its expected observations
Each observation has a pass/fail mark control
Raw JSON is not visible

cases.title: E2E: open plan, mark all observations, verify derived status, add note, verify progress
preconditions: A plan with two cases is loaded (case A two observations, case B one observation)
Git identity is configured on the bench
The review tab is open with all cases not_started

steps.instruction: Click case A in the left pane
Observe the detail-pane status

observations.expected: Status shows not_started
Progress bar shows all cases open

steps.instruction: Mark the first observation of case A pass

observations.expected: The mark is recorded as pass with a timestamp
Case A status updates to in_progress

steps.instruction: Mark the second observation of case A pass

observations.expected: Both observations are pass
Case A derives to passed
Progress bar shows one passed case

steps.instruction: Select case B and mark its observation fail

observations.expected: Observation marked fail with a timestamp
Case B derives to failed
Progress shows one passed and one failed

steps.instruction: Type a note for case B and submit

observations.expected: Note appears as an append-only entry
Note shows author, timestamp, and status-at-write (failed)
No edit or delete controls are present

steps.instruction: Observe the overall progress rollup

observations.expected: Progress shows 1 passed, 1 failed, 0 open
Per-level counts are correct
Orphaned results are excluded

cases.title: Mark an observation pass and verify a timestamp is recorded
preconditions: A plan is loaded with an unmarked case having at least one observation
Git identity is configured
Review tab is open with the case selected

steps.instruction: Locate an unmarked observation in the step detail
Click the pass segment of the mark control

observations.expected: Observation is marked pass
A timestamp is displayed adjacent to the mark
The mark persists on re-render without reload

cases.title: Mark an observation fail and verify a timestamp is recorded
preconditions: A plan is loaded with an unmarked case having at least one observation
Git identity is configured
Review tab is open with the case selected

steps.instruction: Click the fail segment of the mark control on an unmarked observation

observations.expected: Observation is marked fail
A timestamp is displayed
The fail mark is visually distinct from a pass mark

cases.title: Derived case status is not_started when no observations are marked
preconditions: A plan is loaded with a case whose observations are all unmarked
Review tab is open

steps.instruction: Select the case with no marked observations

observations.expected: Status label shows not_started
Status indicator reflects not_started styling

cases.title: Derived case status is in_progress when some but not all observations are marked
preconditions: A plan is loaded with a case that has two observations, both unmarked
Review tab is open with the case selected

steps.instruction: Mark the first observation pass

observations.expected: Status updates to in_progress
Progress bar updates accordingly

steps.instruction: Confirm the second observation remains unmarked

observations.expected: Status remains in_progress (not passed, not failed)

cases.title: Derived case status is passed when all observations are marked pass
preconditions: A plan is loaded with a case having exactly two observations, both unmarked
Review tab is open with the case selected

steps.instruction: Mark both observations pass

observations.expected: Status derives to passed after the final mark
Status indicator updates immediately

cases.title: Derived case status is failed when all observations are marked and at least one fails
preconditions: A plan is loaded with a case having two observations: one marked pass, one unmarked
Review tab is open with the case selected

steps.instruction: Mark the second observation fail

observations.expected: All observations are marked
Status derives to failed (not passed) because at least one mark is fail
Status indicator updates to failed styling

cases.title: Manual status override changes the displayed status and is recorded distinctly from the derived value
preconditions: A plan is loaded with a case in not_started derived status
Review tab is open with the case selected

steps.instruction: Open the status override select
Select blocked

observations.expected: Displayed status changes to blocked
An 'override active' marker is shown
The derived value is preserved internally and does not change

steps.instruction: Observe the case row in the left pane

observations.expected: Status reflects the override value (blocked), not the derived value

cases.title: Append-only note is recorded with author, timestamp, and status-at-write
preconditions: A case is selected with status in_progress
Git identity is configured on the bench
The notes rail is visible

steps.instruction: Focus the add-note field

observations.expected: Input is focused and ready

steps.instruction: Type a note and submit

observations.expected: Note appears as a new entry in the rail
Note shows author from git user.name
Note shows a timestamp near the current time
Note shows status-at-write (in_progress)
Note text matches what was typed

steps.instruction: Observe the note entry

observations.expected: No edit control is present
No delete control is present

cases.title: Progress rollup per level and overall updates as observations are marked
preconditions: A plan is loaded with cases across multiple levels (L1, L2, L3)
All cases are initially not_started
Review tab is open

steps.instruction: Observe the initial progress bar and per-level counts

observations.expected: Overall shows 0 passed, 0 failed, all cases open
Per-level sections show 0 passed, 0 failed

steps.instruction: Mark all observations for one L1 case pass
Observe the progress rollup

observations.expected: L1 passed increments by 1
Overall passed increments by 1
Open decrements by 1

steps.instruction: Mark all observations for one L2 case fail
Observe the progress rollup

observations.expected: L2 failed increments by 1
Overall failed increments by 1
The segmented bar reflects new proportions

cases.title: Progress rollup excludes orphaned results
preconditions: A plan has been executed and results stored
The plan was updated to remove one case, creating an orphaned result
Review tab is open

steps.instruction: Observe the progress rollup counts and bar

observations.expected: The orphaned result (for the removed case) is not counted in passed, failed, or open
Only results for cases currently in the plan are included

cases.title: Manual override takes precedence over derived status when observations are later marked
preconditions: A case has a manual override of blocked set
The case has one unmarked observation

steps.instruction: Mark the observation pass (all observations now pass)
Observe the displayed status

observations.expected: Status still shows blocked (the override), not passed
The override active marker remains visible
The derived value updates internally to passed but is not displayed

cases.title: Submitting a blank note is rejected without creating an entry
preconditions: A case is selected in the detail pane
The notes rail is visible

steps.instruction: Focus the add-note field without typing

observations.expected: Add-note input is focused

steps.instruction: Attempt to submit the empty note

observations.expected: No new note entry is created
The submit control is disabled or an inline validation message prevents submission
Existing notes are unchanged

cases.title: Git identity unset: writes use a sentinel author and the UI shows a warning
preconditions: The bench has no git user.name and no git user.email configured
A case is selected

steps.instruction: Mark an observation pass or fail

observations.expected: The mark write succeeds (does not error or crash)
A UI warning indicates git identity is not set
The mark is attributed to a sentinel author

steps.instruction: Add a note

observations.expected: The note write succeeds
The note author shows the sentinel value, not an empty string
The warning remains visible

cases.title: Observation-mark persist round-trip completes within 150ms
preconditions: A plan is loaded with at least one unmarked case
Typical CI or local-dev conditions
Performance instrumentation is active

steps.instruction: Record the timestamp immediately before clicking a mark control
Click the mark control and wait for the persisted confirmation

observations.expected: The click-to-persisted round-trip completes under 150ms at p95
No UI jank or blocking is observed

cases.title: Virtualised list renders a 500-case plan at p95 under 300ms after data load
preconditions: A synthetic plan of exactly 500 cases across all levels is prepared
A render-timing harness is active
No prior render of this plan is cached

steps.instruction: Trigger data load for the 500-case plan and start timing
Measure the time from data-load completion to the case list being interactive

observations.expected: p95 render time is under 300ms
List virtualisation is active: only a windowed subset of DOM nodes is rendered (not all 500)
Scrolling is smooth without layout thrash

cases.title: Marking observations is fully keyboard operable with visible focus
preconditions: A plan is loaded with at least two cases, each with unmarked observations
Keyboard-only navigation is in use
Review tab is focused

steps.instruction: Tab through the case list rows

observations.expected: Each row receives a visible focus indicator
Focus order is logical

steps.instruction: Activate a case via keyboard and move to the detail pane

observations.expected: Detail pane opens and focus moves to the first interactive element

steps.instruction: Tab to a pass/fail mark control and mark it via keyboard

observations.expected: The mark control shows a visible focus ring
The observation is marked without a mouse
Keyboard activation is equivalent to clicking

cases.title: Review UI produces zero vitest-axe violations on render
preconditions: A plan with several cases across levels is loaded
vitest-axe is configured

steps.instruction: Render the review UI with a populated plan in the test harness
Run vitest-axe against the rendered tree

observations.expected: Zero accessibility violations are reported
WCAG 2.1 AA ruleset is applied

cases.title: Result marks persist across page reload
preconditions: A project is registered and its roubo.yaml is valid
A spec exists at .specifications/<slug>/test-cases.json with at least two cases
TestBench is open for the spec on bench-1
No prior test-results.json exists

steps.instruction: Mark TC-001 pass and TC-002 fail using the result controls
Reload the browser tab
Re-open the TestBench tab for the same spec and bench
Observe the result state

observations.expected: TC-001 shows pass
TC-002 shows fail
No data-loss error or recovery prompt is shown

cases.title: Result sidecar is written beside the spec in the focused project repo, not in ~/.roubo
preconditions: A project is registered with repoPath /path/to/project
A spec slug 'checkout-flow' exists under .specifications/checkout-flow/
TestBench is open for this spec on bench-2

steps.instruction: Mark any case pass
Inspect the filesystem for the created results file

observations.expected: /path/to/project/.specifications/checkout-flow/test-results.json is created
No test-results.json appears anywhere under ~/.roubo

steps.instruction: Verify the file structure

observations.expected: That entry contains result records keyed by case id

cases.title: Source test-cases.json is never mutated when saving results or reconciling
preconditions: A spec with test-cases.json exists under .specifications/my-spec/
The SHA-256 checksum of test-cases.json is recorded before the run

steps.instruction: Mark several cases pass and fail and add notes
Edit the plan to trigger staleness and apply reconcile
Verify the SHA-256 checksum of test-cases.json against the recorded value

observations.expected: Checksum is identical: test-cases.json has zero bytes changed
Only test-results.json was written or modified

cases.title: Staleness banner appears when the source plan content hash changes
preconditions: A spec has test-results.json recorded against a known plan hash
TestBench is open and clean (no banner)

steps.instruction: Edit test-cases.json to add a new case (plan update)
Refresh or re-open the TestBench tab

observations.expected: An amber staleness banner is displayed
The banner indicates the source plan changed

steps.instruction: Observe the banner call-to-action

observations.expected: A 'Reconcile' action button is visible

cases.title: Reconcile dialog lists added, changed, and orphan cases correctly
preconditions: test-cases.json updated: one case added, one title changed, one removed
test-results.json has results for all original cases including the removed one
Staleness banner is visible

steps.instruction: Click 'Reconcile' on the banner
Observe the reconcile dialog

observations.expected: The added case appears in an 'Added' section
The title-changed case appears in a 'Changed' section
The removed case appears in an 'Orphan' section with its marks preserved
No case is silently deleted

steps.instruction: Click 'Apply (keep orphans)'

observations.expected: The added case appears with no prior result
The changed case shows its updated title
The orphaned case is visible as archived with its marks intact
Archived cases are excluded from the rollup
No purge prompt has appeared yet

cases.title: E2E: record results, update plan, detect staleness, reconcile, verify data integrity
preconditions: A project is registered with a valid roubo.yaml
Spec 'auth-flow' has cases TC-A, TC-B, TC-C
TestBench is open for auth-flow on bench-1
No prior test-results.json exists

steps.instruction: Mark TC-A pass, TC-B fail with note 'broken redirect', TC-C pass
Reload the TestBench tab and confirm results persisted

observations.expected: TC-A pass, TC-B fail with note, TC-C pass

steps.instruction: Edit test-cases.json: remove TC-B, add TC-D
Navigate away from and back to the TestBench tab

observations.expected: An amber staleness banner is shown

steps.instruction: Click 'Reconcile' on the banner
Inspect the reconcile dialog

observations.expected: TC-D is listed as Added
TC-B is listed as Orphan with its fail mark and note preserved

steps.instruction: Click 'Apply (keep orphans)'

observations.expected: TC-A and TC-C remain active with their pass marks
TC-D appears active with no mark
TC-B appears archived with its fail mark and note
The rollup counts only TC-A and TC-C
The staleness banner is dismissed

steps.instruction: Inspect .specifications/auth-flow/test-results.json on disk

observations.expected: The file contains a bench-1 key with results for TC-A, TC-B (archived), TC-C
test-cases.json checksum is unchanged

cases.title: Orphaned results are purged only after explicit user confirmation
preconditions: At least one orphaned case exists in the archived section after reconcile

steps.instruction: Locate the archived (orphaned) case
Initiate purge via the purge control
Observe whether the case is removed immediately

observations.expected: The case is NOT immediately removed
A confirmation prompt asks the user to confirm the purge

steps.instruction: Cancel the confirmation

observations.expected: The archived case is still present with its marks intact

steps.instruction: Re-open the purge confirmation and confirm

observations.expected: The archived case is removed from the list
Removal is reflected in test-results.json on disk

cases.title: Whitespace-only change to the source plan does not trigger a false staleness flag
preconditions: test-cases.json has saved results and a stored plan hash
TestBench shows no staleness banner

steps.instruction: Reformat test-cases.json (trailing newline, reindentation) without changing any case content or ids
Reload the TestBench tab
Observe the banner

observations.expected: No staleness banner is shown (the canonicalised hash normalises whitespace/formatting)

cases.title: Corrupt test-results.json causes a fail-open recovery prompt, not a crash
preconditions: A spec has a test-results.json intentionally corrupted with invalid JSON bytes
TestBench is opened for that spec

steps.instruction: Navigate to the TestBench tab for the spec with the corrupt results file

observations.expected: The tab loads without crashing or an unhandled error boundary
A recovery prompt indicates the results file could not be read
The case list still renders from test-cases.json with marks cleared
No data from the corrupt file is partially applied

cases.title: Missing test-results.json yields a clean empty state, not an error
preconditions: A spec has a valid test-cases.json
No test-results.json is present beside it

steps.instruction: Open TestBench for the spec

observations.expected: All cases render with no marks (clean slate)
No error or crash is shown
No recovery prompt is shown (a missing file is a valid initial state)
The user can immediately begin marking

cases.title: Future-schema test-results.json causes a fail-open recovery prompt
preconditions: test-results.json has a schemaVersion higher than the implementation understands
The file is otherwise structurally valid JSON

steps.instruction: Open TestBench for the spec with the future-versioned results file

observations.expected: A recovery prompt indicates the results format is from a newer version
The tab does not crash
Cases render from test-cases.json with marks cleared
The user can discard the unreadable results and start fresh

cases.title: Reconcile orphans a removed case with results, never deletes it
preconditions: Case TC-X has a pass mark and a note recorded in test-results.json
TC-X is then removed from test-cases.json
The staleness banner has appeared

steps.instruction: Click 'Reconcile' on the banner
Observe the orphan section

observations.expected: TC-X is listed as an orphan, not silently absent
TC-X's pass mark and note are displayed

steps.instruction: Click 'Apply (keep orphans)'
Inspect test-results.json

observations.expected: TC-X's entry is still present, tagged archived
The pass mark and note are not deleted

steps.instruction: Inspect the rollup

observations.expected: TC-X is excluded from the pass/fail rollup
TC-X is visible in an archived section and has not been purged

cases.title: Cross-device write (EXDEV) is handled without data loss
preconditions: The repo workspace and the OS temp directory are on different filesystems (simulated)
A result is ready to persist

steps.instruction: Mark a case result, triggering a write of test-results.json
Observe the write when a cross-device rename would raise EXDEV

observations.expected: The write completes using a same-directory temp file then rename strategy
test-results.json contains the expected result
No EXDEV error is surfaced

steps.instruction: Reload TestBench

observations.expected: The marked result is still present

cases.title: Path-traversal slug is rejected: ../ in the slug does not write outside repoPath
preconditions: The results write endpoint accepts a spec slug parameter
A registered project has repoPath /path/to/project

steps.instruction: Send a write request using slug '../../etc/evil' or '../outside-spec'

observations.expected: The server returns a 4xx error
No file is written outside /path/to/project
The error indicates an invalid or disallowed slug

steps.instruction: Confirm via filesystem inspection

observations.expected: Zero new files exist outside /path/to/project

cases.title: Write outside the registered repoPath is rejected by the resolveWithin guard
preconditions: A project is registered with repoPath /workspace/myproject
A second directory /workspace/other exists on the same machine

steps.instruction: Attempt to persist results via a resolved path that escapes repoPath (e.g. a symlink pointing outside)

observations.expected: The server resolves the absolute path and checks it is within /workspace/myproject
If outside, the server returns 403 and does not write

steps.instruction: Verify no file was created outside /workspace/myproject

observations.expected: Zero files written outside the registered repoPath

cases.title: Bench-keyed map isolates results between two benches reviewing the same spec
preconditions: A project has spec 'my-spec' with TC-1, TC-2, TC-3
bench-1 and bench-2 are both active for the project

steps.instruction: On bench-1, mark TC-1 pass and TC-2 fail
On bench-2, mark TC-1 fail and TC-3 pass
Inspect .specifications/my-spec/test-results.json

observations.expected: A 'bench-1' key holds TC-1 pass, TC-2 fail
A 'bench-2' key holds TC-1 fail, TC-3 pass
The two bench entries are independent with no cross-contamination

steps.instruction: Reload TestBench on bench-1

observations.expected: bench-1 still shows TC-1 pass and TC-2 fail, unaffected by bench-2

cases.title: A conforming test-cases.json validates successfully
preconditions: The zod source for the test-cases schema is compiled and exported
A well-formed test-cases.json fixture exists with all required fields populated

steps.instruction: Load the conforming test-cases.json fixture through the validator

observations.expected: Validation returns success with zero errors
The parsed result matches the input fixture

cases.title: A conforming test-results.json referencing valid case ids validates successfully
preconditions: The zod source for the test-results schema is compiled and exported
A well-formed test-results.json fixture references case ids present in the corresponding test-cases.json
The results file requires no edits to test-cases.json to be valid

steps.instruction: Load the conforming test-results.json fixture through the validator

observations.expected: Validation returns success with zero errors
All result entries and referenced case ids are preserved

cases.title: E2E: author schemas, run the generate script, validate output for both files
preconditions: The repo is checked out with dependencies installed
The zod-to-JSON-Schema generate script exists
No prior schema/ output files exist (clean state)

steps.instruction: Run the generate script targeting the test-cases schema
Confirm schema/test-cases.schema.json is written

observations.expected: The file exists
It contains a $id with a semver-versioned URI

steps.instruction: Run the generate script targeting the test-results schema
Confirm schema/test-results.schema.json is written

observations.expected: The file exists
It contains a $id with a semver-versioned URI

steps.instruction: Validate a conforming test-cases.json fixture against the generated test-cases schema
Validate a conforming test-results.json fixture against the generated test-results schema

observations.expected: Both validations pass with zero errors
The results file references case ids without requiring edits to test-cases.json

cases.title: Validation surfaces an actionable error when test-cases.json is missing a required field
preconditions: The validator is initialised with the published test-cases schema
A malformed fixture omits the required 'title' field from one case

steps.instruction: Load the malformed test-cases.json fixture

observations.expected: Validation fails
The error names the missing field ('title')
The error identifies the affected case by index or id
No opaque or unhandled exception is thrown

cases.title: Validation flags a test-results.json referencing a non-existent case id
preconditions: The validator is initialised
A test-results.json fixture references case id 'TC-999' not present in the known test-cases corpus

steps.instruction: Load the fixture with the test-cases corpus provided

observations.expected: Validation returns a failure or warning
The error names the unresolvable id ('TC-999')
The orphaned result entry is not silently discarded

cases.title: A test-cases.json that omits all optional targeting fields still validates
preconditions: The validator is initialised
A fixture has no case using the optional 'target' or 'observe' targeting fields

steps.instruction: Load the fixture

observations.expected: Validation returns success
The absence of optional targeting fields is not an error

cases.title: A test-cases.json with per-step target and per-observation observe fields validates
preconditions: The validator is initialised
A fixture has at least one step with a 'target' (CSS selector, ARIA role + name, visible-text anchor, route context) and one observation with an 'observe' target

steps.instruction: Load the fixture

observations.expected: Validation returns success
All targeting sub-fields are accepted
No error is raised for any combination of CSS selector, ARIA role + name, visible-text, route, or region

cases.title: The in-app UI ignores an unknown optional field and does not error
preconditions: The in-app TestBench UI is running
A test-cases.json fixture contains an unknown optional field not present in the current schema

steps.instruction: Load the fixture with the unknown optional field into the in-app UI
Inspect the UI rendering of the affected case

observations.expected: The case renders without error or crash
The unknown field is ignored in the display
No validation error is surfaced to the user for the unknown optional field

cases.title: A test-cases.json authored under schema v1.0 still validates after a minor version bump
preconditions: A backward-compat corpus contains a test-cases.json valid under schema v1.0
The schema has been updated to a v1.x minor version (additive only)
The new validator is initialised

steps.instruction: Load the v1.0 corpus through the v1.x validator

observations.expected: Validation succeeds for all fixtures
Zero previously-valid files are now rejected
No breaking change is introduced by the minor bump

cases.title: A breaking schema change requires a major bump and a documented migration
preconditions: A breaking change to a schema has been proposed (a required field renamed or removed)
The schema $id has been updated

steps.instruction: Inspect the $id of the updated schema

observations.expected: The major version segment of the semver in $id has incremented

steps.instruction: Check for a migration document covering existing test-results.json files

observations.expected: A migration guide describes how to update files from the prior major version
It is referenced from the changelog or release notes

steps.instruction: Validate a prior-major test-results.json against the new schema

observations.expected: Validation fails with a clear version-mismatch error
The error references the migration guide

cases.title: CI drift guard fails the build when checked-in JSON Schema differs from the zod source
preconditions: The generate script and CI drift guard are configured
The zod source has been edited to add a field
The schema/ JSON Schema files have NOT been regenerated (drift)

steps.instruction: Trigger the CI drift guard (regenerate then git-diff against checked-in files)

observations.expected: The step detects a diff between generated output and checked-in files
The build fails with a non-zero exit code
The log identifies the drifted schema file(s)

cases.title: A test-results.json write validates against the schema before persisting
preconditions: The results writer is initialised
A results payload is prepared that is non-conforming (a missing required field)

steps.instruction: Invoke the test-results.json write with the non-conforming payload

observations.expected: The write is rejected before any data is written to disk
An actionable error identifies the schema violation
Any existing test-results.json is not overwritten or corrupted

cases.title: TestBench tab appears in app settings and the toggle defaults to enabled
preconditions: Roubo is running
No prior UserPreferences entry for the TestBench toggle exists (fresh state)

steps.instruction: Open app settings
Click the 'TestBench' tab

observations.expected: A 'TestBench' tile is shown with a label, description, and a switch
The switch is ON (enabled) by default
No 'Disabled' helper text is shown

cases.title: Toggling TestBench off hides the create option and feature surface
preconditions: Roubo is running
TestBench is currently enabled (toggle ON)

steps.instruction: Open app settings and navigate to the 'TestBench' tab
Click the switch to toggle TestBench OFF

observations.expected: The switch turns OFF (stone track)
Helper text 'Disabled. The create-TestBench option and the TestBench surface are hidden.' appears

steps.instruction: Close settings and inspect the main UI

observations.expected: The create-TestBench option is no longer visible
The TestBench feature surface is no longer accessible

cases.title: Toggling TestBench back on restores the create option and surface
preconditions: Roubo is running
TestBench is currently disabled (toggle OFF)

steps.instruction: Open app settings and navigate to the 'TestBench' tab
Click the switch to toggle TestBench ON

observations.expected: The switch turns ON (amber)
The disabled helper text is no longer shown

steps.instruction: Close settings and inspect the main UI

observations.expected: The create-TestBench option is visible
The TestBench feature surface is accessible

cases.title: E2E: toggle TestBench off, verify hidden, toggle on, verify restored
preconditions: Roubo is running
TestBench is enabled (toggle ON)
The main UI shows the create-TestBench option and surface

steps.instruction: Open app settings
Navigate to the 'TestBench' tab
Observe the switch state

observations.expected: Switch is ON (amber) with no disabled helper text

steps.instruction: Click the switch to toggle OFF

observations.expected: Switch turns OFF
The disabled helper text appears

steps.instruction: Close app settings and inspect the main UI

observations.expected: The create-TestBench option is absent
The TestBench surface is not accessible

steps.instruction: Re-open settings, navigate to the 'TestBench' tab, and toggle ON

observations.expected: Switch turns ON
The disabled helper text is removed

steps.instruction: Close settings and inspect the main UI

observations.expected: The create-TestBench option is visible again
The TestBench feature surface is accessible again

cases.title: Toggle state persists in UserPreferences across an app restart
preconditions: Roubo is running
TestBench is currently enabled

steps.instruction: Open app settings, navigate to 'TestBench', and toggle OFF
Verify the preference is written to UserPreferences (not settings.json)

observations.expected: The UserPreferences store records the TestBench toggle as false
No TestBench entry exists in any settings.json

steps.instruction: Restart Roubo
Open app settings and the 'TestBench' tab

observations.expected: The switch is OFF, reflecting the persisted preference
The disabled helper text is shown

steps.instruction: Inspect the main UI without changing the setting

observations.expected: The create-TestBench option remains hidden after restart
The surface remains inaccessible

cases.title: Toggling off while a TestBench bench is open hides the create option
preconditions: Roubo is running
TestBench is enabled
A TestBench exists and its detail view is open

steps.instruction: With the TestBench detail open, navigate to app settings 'TestBench'
Toggle the switch OFF

observations.expected: Switch transitions to OFF
The disabled helper text appears

steps.instruction: Return to the main UI

observations.expected: The create-TestBench option is hidden
The TestBench surface entry point is no longer rendered

cases.title: TestBench switch is keyboard operable via Space/Enter with announced state
preconditions: Roubo is running
App settings 'TestBench' tab is open
Keyboard-only navigation is in use

steps.instruction: Tab to the TestBench switch

observations.expected: The switch receives a visible focus indicator
A screen reader announces the switch label and current state

steps.instruction: Press Space to toggle OFF

observations.expected: The switch transitions to OFF
The new state is announced
The disabled helper text becomes visible

steps.instruction: Press Space again to toggle ON

observations.expected: The switch transitions to ON
The new state is announced

cases.title: TestBench toggle is stored in UserPreferences, never in settings.json
preconditions: Roubo is running
App settings 'TestBench' tab is open

steps.instruction: Toggle the TestBench switch OFF
Inspect all settings.json files in the project and ~/.roubo/

observations.expected: No TestBench toggle key exists in any settings.json
The change is recorded only in the UserPreferences store

cases.title: Validate plus staleness-hash of a ~1MB plan completes within 200ms
preconditions: A synthetic ~1MB test-cases.json plan fixture is prepared
The schema validator and the canonicalize-plus-hash path are available
A benchmark harness is active

steps.instruction: Load the ~1MB plan fixture and record the start timestamp
Run schema validation followed by the canonicalised staleness hash over the plan

observations.expected: The combined validate-plus-hash duration is under 200ms at p95
The computed plan hash is stable across repeated runs of the same input
